description_long: This bungalow is a single-story residential building with a sprawling, cross-shaped footprint and a prominent central section. The roof is a complex hip style, featuring multiple ridges and slopes that extend over the main and side wings. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely finished with stucco or siding, and the roof is clad in a uniform, muted gray material. The front entrance is recessed under a small gabled portico, flanked by evenly spaced windows that provide a balanced, symmetrical appearance. The structure sits on a flat, open lot, with no visible landscaping or fencing. The overall design suggests a modern ranch or contemporary bungalow style, optimized for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A single-story, cross-shaped bungalow with a complex hip roof, light exterior walls, and a recessed front entrance under a small gabled portico.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance under a small gabled portico and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cross-shaped layout and the extended hip roof sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full width of the bungalow and additional windows along the back facade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the depth of the central section and the uniform roof design.
